title: An assembly feeder and a method for automated assembly of a primer device and a detonator unit
abstract: An assembly feeder(1) comprising a magazine unit (2) comprising a primer magazine (4) adapted to receive primer devices (20) containing an explosive substance and a detonator magazine (6) adapted to receive detonator units (30) comprising a signal transmitter(32) and a detonator device (34) connected to the signal transmitter. Also described is amethod for automated assembly ofa primer device(20)and a detonator unit(30), which primer device (20) contains an explosive substance and which detonator unit(30)comprises a signal transmitter (32) and a detonator device (34) connected to the signal transmitter, the method comprising loading primer devices (20) in a primer magazine (4) in a magazine unit (2), loading detonator units (30) in a detonator magazine (6) in the magazine unit (2), transporting the loaded magazine unit (2) to a charging site, and performing an automated assembly of a primer device (20) and a detonator unit (30) in the magazine unit (2) thereby forming a detonating package (40).The assembled detonating package (40) is then presented to be pickable by an industrial robot at the charging site.
claims: Claims Patent Claims 1. An assembly feeder (1 ) comprising a magazine unit (2) comprising a primer magazine (4) adapted to receive primer devices (20) containing an explosive substance and a detonator magazine (6) adapted to receive detonator units (30) comprising a signal transmitter (32) and a detonator device (34) connected to the signal transmitter. 2. The assembly feeder according to claim 1 , wherein it comprises an assembling mechanism configured to assemble a primer device (20) and a detonator unit (30) by inserting the detonator device (34) of the detonator unit into the primer device (20) and thereby obtaining a detonating package (40). 3. The assembly feeder according to claim 2, wherein it comprises a presenting device that makes the detonating package (40) pickable by an industrial robot. 4. The assembly feeder according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the primer magazine (4) comprises an individual primer holder space (14) for each primer device (20). 5. The assembly feeder according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the detonator magazine (6) comprises an individual detonator holder space (16) for each detonator unit (30). 6. The assembly feeder according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein it comprises an alignment arrangement adapted for placing a primer device (20) in a position in alignment with a detonator unit (30), or vice versa, in preparation for assembly of the primer device and the detonator unit. 7. The assembly feeder according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the signal transmitter (32) is stored in the detonator magazine (6) in a rolled up configuration forming a roll of signal transmitter such that the signal transmitter can be gradually unrolled from the roll when a detonating package (40), comprising a detonator unit (30) assembled with a primer device (20), is picked by a robot. 8. The assembly feeder according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the primer magazine (4) and/or the detonator magazine (6) is a rotary magazine. 9. The assembly feeder according to any one of the preceding claims, wherein the primer magazine (4) and/or the detonator magazine (6) is a linear magazine. 10. A method for automated assembly of a primer device (20) and a detonator unit (30), which primer device (20) contains an explosive substance and which detonator unit (30) comprises a signal transmitter (32) and a detonator device (34) connected to the signal transmitter, the method comprising loading primer devices (20) in a primer magazine (4) in a magazine unit (2), loading detonator units (30) in a detonator magazine (6) in the magazine unit (2), transporting the loaded magazine unit (2) to a charging site, and performing an automated assembly of a primer device (20) and a detonator unit (30) in the magazine unit (2) thereby forming a detonating package (40). 1 1. The method according to claim 10 wherein it comprises presenting the assembled detonating package (40) to be pickable by an industrial robot at the charging site. 12. The method according to any one of claims 10-1 1 , wherein it comprises loading each primer device (20) in an individual primer holder space (14) forming part of the primer magazine (4). 13. The method according to any one of claims 10-12, wherein it comprises loading each detonator unit (30) in an individual detonator holder space (16) forming part of the detonator magazine (6). 14. The method according to claim 13, wherein it comprises loading the detonator units (30) in their respective individual detonator holder spaces (16) in a predetermined order depending on delay times for the detonator units. 15. The method according to any one of claims 10-14, wherein it comprises placing a primer device (20) in a position in alignment with a detonator unit (30), or vice versa, in preparation for automated assembly of the primer device and the detonator unit. 16. The method according to any one of claims 10-15, wherein it comprises storing the signal transmitter (32) in the detonator magazine (6) in a rolled up configuration forming a roll of signal transmitter and gradually unrolling the signal transmitter (32) from the roll when the detonating package (40) is picked by a robot. 17. The method according to any one of claims 10-16, wherein the step of transporting the loaded magazine unit (2) to the charging site is performed by transporting the loaded magazine unit on a mobile robot platform.
